demoInstance.scope: The factor animation is a fixed, reproducible successful replay for N=55 under model A with one QFT separation layer omitted. Aggregate recovery statements refer separately to the frozen eight-semiprime holdout.

components.label: Exponent x₁
components.what: A five-qubit exponent register representing integers x₁ from 0 through M−1, with M=32 in this demo.
components.role: It controls the exponent of circuit base a₁=4. The arithmetic map depends on 4ˣ¹ modulo 55.
components.built: build_arbitrary_base_circuit() creates one M-sized quantum register for each modular base.

components.label: Exponent x₂
components.what: A second independent five-qubit exponent register representing x₂ from 0 through 31.
components.role: It controls the exponent of circuit base a₂=9, so the full arithmetic label depends on both exponent coordinates.
components.built: The same circuit builder creates x₂ separately; the two registers are transformed and measured independently.

components.label: Hadamards x₁
components.what: One Hadamard on every qubit of x₁.
components.role: It creates a uniform hard-box superposition in the notebook circuit, allowing many exponents to be evaluated coherently.
components.built: The implementation calls circuit.h(x) independently for every exponent register before modular arithmetic.

components.label: Hadamards x₂
components.what: One Hadamard on every qubit of x₂.
components.role: Together with x₁, it spans a two-dimensional box of exponent combinations.
components.built: The builder applies the same verified preparation to x₂, preserving register separation and measurement order.

components.label: Result register y
components.what: A six-qubit register large enough to encode residues modulo N=55.
components.role: It is initialized to |1⟩ and accumulates the modular product controlled by x₁ and x₂.
components.built: The builder allocates N.bit_length() qubits and applies X to y[0], producing the multiplicative identity.

components.label: Auxiliary workspace
components.what: Seven temporary qubits used by reversible modular arithmetic.
components.role: Intermediate carries and reversible arithmetic need workspace that must be returned to a clean state before Fourier sampling.
components.built: The circuit allocates n+1 auxiliary qubits and passes them to each audited external modular-exponentiation gate.

components.label: Mod exp a₁
components.what: A reversible controlled modular-exponentiation block for circuit base a₁=4.
components.role: It multiplies the shared result register by 4ˣ¹ modulo 55 without measuring x₁.
components.built: The implementation imports and audits modular_exponentiation_gate(), then wires x₁, y, and aux in a fixed order.

components.label: Mod exp a₂
components.what: The second reversible modular-exponentiation block, now controlled by x₂.
components.role: Sequential action on the shared result register completes the product 4ˣ¹9ˣ² modulo 55.
components.built: The builder loops over the stored base list, preserving which exponent register controls which arithmetic block.
components.codeSymbol: for x, a in zip(x_registers, bases)

components.label: Inverse QFT x₁
components.what: A five-qubit inverse quantum Fourier transform on x₁.
components.role: It converts phase structure created by modular-product fibers into a measurable Fourier coordinate.
components.built: The exact circuit uses QFTGate.inverse(); the audited approximation deletes only declared small-angle controlled phases.

components.label: Inverse QFT x₂
components.what: A separate five-qubit inverse QFT on x₂.
components.role: Regev-style sampling is multidimensional: each exponent coordinate receives its own Fourier transform.
components.built: Tests check sign, swaps, endian ordering, unitarity, every cutoff, and exact full-cutoff equivalence.

components.label: Measure x₁
components.what: Computational-basis measurement of the first Fourier register.
components.role: It produces the first coordinate of a classical sample; it does not directly reveal a factor.
components.built: Both exponent registers are mapped into one classical register with tested coordinate and bitstring decoding.

components.label: Measure x₂
components.what: Computational-basis measurement of the second Fourier register.
components.role: Together the two coordinates form one structured Fourier sample consumed by the lattice stage.
components.built: The post-processing receives ordered integer pairs, builds the exact cleared augmented lattice, and verifies recovered relations.

exact.label: Exact QFT
exact.scope: For two five-qubit IQFTs: 20 controlled phases, 52 transpiled CX, depth 36 in the frozen QFT-only accounting.

omit1.label: Truncated QFT — omit 1 layer
omit1.certificate: Not certified by the original worst-case bound
omit1.observed: Met the frozen 0.10 non-inferiority rule in all six model-by-M cells
omit1.scope: Eight held-out semiprimes; at M=32 this includes both models A and B, with N—not repeat batches—as the generalization unit. This does not prove universal safety.

omit2.label: Truncated QFT — omit 2 layers
omit2.certificate: Not certified by the original worst-case bound
omit2.observed: At M=32, met the frozen rule for finite discrete-Gaussian model B but failed it for uniform hard-box model A
omit2.scope: The model-B difference was −0.01758 (CI lower bound −0.03516); the model-A CI crossed the −0.10 margin. Not a universal safety claim.

successfulReplay.label: Fixed successful replay — not a live-success guarantee

latticePipeline.label: Build augmented lattice
latticePipeline.detail: Clear sample denominators and embed all seven Fourier samples into the exact 9×9 integer basis used by this d=2, m=7 replay.

latticePipeline.label: Run LLL
latticePipeline.detail: Run SymPy LLL with δ=3/4 and inspect the implemented Claim-5.1 candidate prefix.

latticePipeline.label: Recover relation z
latticePipeline.detail: Lift z=(3,−1) and check 4³·9⁻¹≡1 (mod 55) exactly; shortness alone is not enough.

latticePipeline.label: Classify L₀ or L\L₀
latticePipeline.detail: Use the permanently stored roots b₁=2 and b₂=3: β=21, β²=1, and β≠±1, so z lies in L\L₀.

latticePipeline.label: Extract factors
latticePipeline.detail: Compute gcd(20,55)=5 and gcd(22,55)=11, then verify 5·11=55.
